folio.label: 190r
folio.l: Than euäe Catoun , was , so moot I the
That al the reuers seyn , of his sentence
And han wel founden , by ex¹ience
That dremes ben , significaciouns
As wel of ioye , as tribulaciouns
That folk enduren , in this lyf päsent
There nedetú make of this , noon argument
The verray preeue , shewetú it in dede
Oon of the grettest auctour , that men rede
Seitú thus , æ whilom tweye felawes wente
On pilgémage , in a ful good entente
And happed so , æ they cam in a toun
Where there was swicú , a congregacou~
Of peeple , and eke so streyt , of herbergage
That they ne founde , as moche as a cotage
In whicú they mygút , I logged be

note.notetxt: 171 There is a small cross next to this line. RJ

folio.l: Wherfore they musten , of necessite
As for æ nygút , de¹ten compaignye
And ecú of hem gootú , to his hostelrie
And took his loggynge , as it wolde falle
That oon of hem was logged , in a stalle
Fer in a ©erd , witú Oxen of the plow
That oæä man was logged , wel y now
As was his auenture , or his fortune
That vs gouänetú , as in comune
¢ And so bifel , æ longe or it was day
This man mette in his bed , æäe as he lay
How æ his felawe , gan vp oon hi~ calle
And seide allas , for in an Oxes stalle
This nygút I shal be mordred , æere I lye
Now helpe me dere brother , or I dye
In alle haste , come to me he seyde
This man out of his sleep~ , for fere abreyde
But whan æ he was wakned , of his sleep~
He turned hi~ , & took of this no keep~
Hi~ thougút his dreem , nas but a vanytee
Thus twyes in his dreem, sleep~ ,dreem, sleep~ , drem~ed he
And at the thridde tyme , ©et his felawe
Cam as hi~ æougút , & seide , I am now slawe
Byholde my blody woundes , depe & wyde
Arys vp erly , in the morwe tyde
And at the west gate , of the Toun q¶ he
A Carte ful of donge , æäe shalt æè se
In whicú my body is hid , ful preuyly
